Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

SBQuantum and Spire to send quantum diamond magnetometer into orbit

SAN FRANCISCO – Canadian startup SBQuantum plans to send a quantum diamond magnetometer into low-Earth orbit March 30 on a Spire Global satellite flying on a SpaceX Falcon 9 rideshare. Spire is providing the satellite, ground stations and data processing for SBQuantum’s magnetometer, which is...

Quantum sensing technology entering orbit enables unprecedented magnetic field measurements for Earth observation and space weather monitoring applications.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

ispace redesigns lunar lander, introduces lunar communications service

Japanese company ispace is revising its lunar lander design and further delaying the first mission by its American subsidiary while also unveiling plans for a lunar satellite constellation.

ispace's shift toward offering lunar communications infrastructure as a service indicates the emerging business model for sustained lunar operations extends beyond cargo delivery to essential orbital utilities.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Qantas Scraps Nonstop Perth-Paris Route In Major European Network Shake-Up

Qantas shakes up its international network to offer more flights to Europe via Singapore.

Qantas is pivoting from ultra-long-haul to a hub-and-spoke model through Singapore, likely to increase aircraft utilization and frequency rather than pursuing distance records.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 27, 2026

Lockheed Martin F-35 workers open contract talks in Fort Worth

The union representing around 5,000 workers building the F-35 at Lockheed Martin’s Fort Worth, Texas, plant has opened…...

Labor negotiations at a critical F-35 production facility could impact military aircraft delivery timelines and defense contractor supply chain stability.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

The Striking Differences Between The MD-11 & DC-10

Though closely related, the MD-11 and DC-10 differed in range, cockpit design, aerodynamics, and ultimately commercial success.

The MD-11's advanced avionics and aerodynamic refinements couldn't overcome early reliability issues that damaged its market reputation against the proven DC-10 platform.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

Psychological safety and employee empowerment in safety reporting directly reduce incident escalation risk and operational disruptions.

London, England — TEKEVER, a leading provider of advanced AI-based autonomous systems, and Quadsat have successfully...

Integration of specialized EW payloads onto commercial drone platforms expands military ISR capabilities while democratizing advanced reconnaissance technology beyond traditional aerospace contractors.

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

The US Domestic Routes Operated By Widebody Aircraft

Some of the country's busiest domestic routes are operated by widebodies.

Widebody deployment on domestic routes indicates airlines are optimizing high-density transcontinental markets where premium seating and cargo capacity generate stronger unit economics than narrowbody alternatives.

Safety Mar 27, 2026

United Airlines Jet & Military Helicopter Separated By Only 500 Feet Over Orange County

A close call in the sunny skies of SoCal.

The incident highlights critical gaps in airspace coordination between civilian and military operations, potentially revealing inadequate communication protocols in shared Class B airspace.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

Satellite Spots a Spawn

The activity of herring around Vancouver Island in British Columbia brightened coastal waters enough to be detectable from space.

Satellite remote sensing capabilities now extend to detecting biological phenomena like fish spawning, demonstrating aerospace technology's expanding role in environmental monitoring and ocean resource management.

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective safety risk management requires organizations to document why residual risks are acceptable after controls are implemented, not just identify hazards.

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Here’s How Much Range The Airbus A350-1000 Has Compared To The Boeing 777-300ER

The Airbus A350-1000 and Boeing 777-300ER are long-haul aviation titans, but the A350's slight edge in range may not be as decisive as it seems.

While the A350-1000's modest range advantage enables marginally longer routes, the 777-300ER's proven reliability and lower operating costs often provide better commercial value for most long-haul operators.

See Simple Flying →

eVTOL Mar 27, 2026

AIR claims $1 billion in orders as eVTOL maker pushes personal aircraft

Israel-based electric aircraft developer AIR said it has surpassed $1 billion in orders, driven by orders for its…...

AIR's $1B order milestone signals growing investor confidence in near-term eVTOL commercialization despite ongoing certification and infrastructure hurdles.

Commercial Mar 27, 2026

Here’s How Much Larger The GE9X Is Compared To The GE90

The engine is set to enter commercial service on the upcoming Boeing 777X.

The GE9X's incremental size increases translate to significant thrust and efficiency gains that will define next-generation widebody economics and long-range capability.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

U.S. GSSAP satellites execute GEO handoff to monitor China’s Shijian-29 spacecraft

Commercial space tracking data shows U.S. satellites coordinating maneuvers to maintain proximity and continuous observation of a pair of Chinese spacecraft in geostationary orbit.

U.S. space surveillance capabilities are evolving to track Chinese orbital activities in real-time, raising implications for airspace safety and geopolitical intelligence gathering.

The San Francisco Bay area is complicated airspace, and right in the middle of it is San Carlos Airport. San Carlos airport is surrounded by terrain, San Francisco International class bravo,...

San Carlos Airport's noise-sensitive location within San Francisco's complex airspace requires pilots to master specialized departure procedures balancing terrain constraints with community relations.

Half of the ten longest routes started in 2025 or will do so in 2026. See them all here!

United's aggressive expansion of ultra-long 737 MAX routes signals carrier confidence in the aircraft's reliability and market demand for premium nonstop connections over traditional hub-and-spoke models.

I'm A Flight Attendant, And These Are The Things I Wish Every Passenger Knew Before Boarding

Practical advice for passengers that makes flights easier.

Flight attendants' operational insights reveal that passenger compliance with pre-flight briefings and crew instructions directly impacts cabin safety protocols and on-time performance.

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

New Route & 9 More Boosted: JetBlue Keeps Turning Up The Heat In Fort Lauderdale

JetBlue adds Cleveland and boosts nine routes as Fort Lauderdale growth accelerates again.

JetBlue's simultaneous expansion of a new route plus nine existing routes signals accelerating demand recovery at Fort Lauderdale, positioning the carrier to capture market share during peak travel season.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

The Aircraft Set To Replace Delta Air Lines' Iconic Boeing 717

Delta's 717 fleet is soon to be retired, and the airline will be replacing the type's operations with the Airbus A220 family.

Delta's A220 replacement offers 20% better fuel efficiency and lower maintenance costs, positioning the airline to recapture profitability on regional routes where the 717 currently operates.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 27, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strikes remain a leading fatal hazard despite pilots' overconfidence in reaction time, requiring enhanced detection systems and lower-altitude operational protocols.

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Space Command begins phased move to Alabama

The new headquarters building is expected to be completed by 2031...

U.S. Space Command's relocation to Alabama represents a significant shift in aerospace infrastructure concentration, with implications for regional defense contracting and STEM workforce development through 2031.

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

What’s Up: April 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA

Mercury shines at its brightest for the year, the Lyrid meteor shower peaks, and a bright new comet makes an appearance in April’s night sky.

April 2026 offers optimal viewing conditions for three celestial events simultaneously, providing rare opportunities for public engagement with astronomy and potential STEM education initiatives.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

La NASA presentará el telescopio Roman completo y ofrecerá una conferencia de prensa

Se invita a los medios de comunicación el martes 21 de abril al Centro de Vuelo Espacial Goddard de la NASA en Greenbelt, Maryland, para conocer el telescopio espacial Nancy Grace Roman de la agencia, cuya construcción terminó recientemente y el cual se encuentra ultimando las pruebas previas a su...

NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope completion marks a critical milestone for next-generation astronomical capabilities with implications for space-based observation infrastructure.

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Government use of commercial procurement models has limitations in space

While government agencies are increasingly turning to commercial contracting mechanisms for space capabilities, that does not necessarily translate to additional business opportunities for the companies offering them.

Government space contracts using commercial models may not expand contractor revenue opportunities despite increased utilization of these procurement mechanisms.

See SpaceNews →

Industry Mar 27, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational efficiency improvements across integrated airline-airport-flight systems offer a faster path to emissions reduction than waiting for alternative fuel or electric aircraft adoption.

See MDPI →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA to Unveil Complete Roman Telescope, Host Media Briefing

Media are invited Tuesday, April 21, to NASA’s Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Maryland, for a look at the agency’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, which recently completed construction and is wrapping up prelaunch testing. This will be one of the last opportunities to view the fully...

Roman telescope's completion marks the final phase before launch, offering NASA a critical public engagement window ahead of deployment.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA Releases Artemis II Moon Mission Launch Countdown

Before NASA sends its astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen on their Artemis II mission around the Moon, the launch team at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida and teams across the country will begin counting down about...

NASA's Artemis II lunar flyby represents the critical validation flight for human deep-space operations before landing missions, with international crew participation signaling expanded exploration partnerships.

See NASA →

Navi AI's public launch signals investor confidence in AI-driven pilot training as airlines seek cost-effective alternatives to traditional flight instruction methods.

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Station Nation: Aaron Rose, Cold Stowage Mission Manager

As a member of the Crew and Thermal Systems Division, Aaron Rose supports critical cargo resupply missions to the International Space Station. In this role, he works with payload developers to safely transport temperature-controlled science experiments to and from station with portable coolers,...

Specialized thermal logistics expertise is critical for ISS cargo missions, requiring coordination between ground systems engineers and payload developers to maintain experiment integrity during transport.

Turning growth into profits remains a challenge as space demand grows

While the space economy is expanding across multiple fronts, questions remain about how some of its most promising opportunities could translate growing demand into sustainable profits.

Space industry players face profitability pressure despite expanding markets, signaling potential consolidation or margin compression ahead for aerospace suppliers.

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Airbus Tests Multi-Aircraft Aerial Firefighting System

Trial linked aircraft, drones and ground crews for enhanced targeting and coordination.

Integrated drone-manned aircraft coordination could significantly improve wildfire suppression efficiency by enabling real-time target sharing and simultaneous multi-vector attacks.

Russian aviation blogger Pavel Koshkin was killed when the Alto NG aircraft he was flying crashed near Kolomna,…...

Misidentification risks in conflict zones are forcing civilian aviators to reconsider flight operations near active military theaters where air defense systems operate with heightened sensitivity.

Xona raises $170 million for satellite navigation network

The company is scaling up production as it looks to build a 258-satellite network to provide positioning, navigation and timing services...

Xona's satellite constellation will create independent PNT capabilities that reduce aviation and critical infrastructure reliance on GPS, addressing emerging resilience requirements.

House Committee Advances ALERT Act

Measure includes collision-mitigation requirements, changes for military aircraft operations and limits on some ADS-B data use.

The legislation introduces operational guardrails around ADS-B transparency, balancing safety mandates with military operational security concerns.
